steps.title: Spot the key connection
steps.content: The strongest clue is so clear that it was almost invisible—clarity is directly linked to invisibility. The only way something becomes nearly invisible is if you can see straight through it, meaning “clear” describes optical transparency.

steps.title: Apply the meaning to the choices
steps.content: “Transparent” is the answer: water this transparent becomes nearly invisible because you see straight through it. Simple, understandable, and obvious describe qualities unrelated to optical transparency. D is correct.
